components.name: metadata_request_setup
components.description: Build CXL read descriptor for N summaries

components.name: llc_pollution_overhead
components.description: LLC misses from metadata: 16 cand * 40% miss * 80 ns
components.source: Intel SDM, Sapphire Rapids LLC miss = 70-90 ns

components.name: cpu_scoring_base
components.description: Fixed scoring setup: branch targets, weight loads
components.source: modeled from ODUS-X featurization (5 features * 3 ops)

components.name: dma_descriptor_build
components.description: Driver: build 8 DMA descriptors * 2 us each
components.source: Linux DMA engine docs: 1.5-3 us per descriptor

sources.note: longer context = more KV fetch, similar slack
large model, more compute slack but also more KV

requirements.why: each request has its own query trajectory
requirements.cost: S * dim bytes per active stream (S = #sequences)

requirements.why: sketch must match the token being decoded
requirements.cost: 4 bytes / sequence; CE must read before scoring
requirements.mitigation: CE blocks scoring until version current

requirements.why: each transformer layer has distinct attention pattern
requirements.mitigation: share sketches across layers (trade-off: Fix 2 shows -0.045 recovery vs per-head)

requirements.why: when sequences are added/removed mid-batch, sketch slots must be remapped
rejected speculative tokens invalidate sketches generated from them
requirements.cost: rollback sketch version counter, re-score candidates
requirements.mitigation: CE must support version rewind; sticky TTL caps damage

requirements.why: sketch should reflect the attention group that will query it
requirements.risk: single sketch averages across heads, loses signal

requirements.why: evicted request's sketch slot must not leak to new request
requirements.risk: privacy (stale sketch readable by next tenant)

old_vs_new.old_claim: A cascaded scorer reduces false admits by sqrt(2) via repeated scoring.
old_vs_new.problem: Assumes independent noise. Deterministic delta = 0 (v1 Exp 3).
old_vs_new.new_claim: A cascaded scorer refines admission by introducing Round-2 evidence (HBM-resident KV or a second independent feature set), NOT by rescoring the same summary.

old_vs_new.old_claim: Round 2 re-scores survivors using the same summary, reducing variance.
old_vs_new.problem: Only true if Round-2 noise is independent. Reviewers will catch this.
old_vs_new.new_claim: Round 2 uses evidence unavailable in Round 1: (a) partial HBM KV for already-promoted chunks, or (b) a secondary scorer trained on different features. The cascade is an ensemble, not a retrial.

old_vs_new.old_claim: Cascade improves recovery from 0.78 -> 0.85 (independent noise assumption).
old_vs_new.problem: Fresh-noise simulation; reviewers reject as synthetic.
old_vs_new.new_claim: Cascade improvement decomposition: (a) deterministic = 0 pp (v2 result), (b) correlated-error rho=1.0 = 0 pp (ceiling), (c) with actual HBM-evidence Round-2 = [X] pp (needs real-trace measurement).

old_vs_new.old_claim: CE pipelines Round-1 and Round-2 to hide latency.
old_vs_new.problem: No pipelining benefit if Round-2 has no new evidence.
old_vs_new.new_claim: When cascade is ENABLED (optional feature), CE pipelines Round-2 scoring behind Round-1 DMA. When DISABLED, CE uses single-round admission with equivalent PROSE performance on our benchmarks.

old_vs_new.problem: Reviewers will ask why cascade helps if scorer is deterministic.
old_vs_new.new_claim: We explicitly note that rescoring identical evidence provides no benefit. Cascade requires evidence diversity (Round-2 observes new information such as partial KV payload) to improve over single-round admission.
